name: Peace Domain
description: The balm of peace thrives at the heart of healthy communities, between friendly nations, and in the souls of the kindhearted. The gods of peace inspire people of all sorts to resolve conflict and to stand up against those forces that try to prevent peace from flourishing. Clerics of the Peace Domain preside over the signing of treaties, and they are often asked to arbitrate in disputes. These clerics' blessings draw people together and help them shoulder one another's burdens, and the clerics' magic aids those who are driven to fight for the way of peace.

features.name: Peace Domain Spells
features.description: Your connection to this divine domain ensures you always have certain spells ready. When you reach a Cleric level specified in the Peace Domain Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Implement of Peace
features.description: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Insight}, {@skill Performance}, or {@skill Persuasion} skill (your choice).

features.name: Emboldening Bond
features.description: You can forge an empowering bond among people who are at peace with one another. As an action, you choose a number of willing creatures within 30 feet of you (this can include yourself) up to your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}. You create a magical bond among them for 10 minutes or until you use this feature again. While any bonded creature is within 30 feet of another bonded creature, the creature can roll a {@dice d4} and add the number rolled to an attack roll, an ability check, or a saving throw it makes. Each creature can add the {@dice d4} no more than once per turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Balm of Peace
features.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to make your very presence a soothing balm. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can move up to your speed, without provoking opportunity attacks, and when you move within 5 feet of any other creature during this action, you can restore a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to that creature equal to {@dice 2d6} plus your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1 hit point). A creature can receive this healing only once whenever you take this action.

features.name: Protective Bond
features.description: The bond you forge between people helps them protect each other. When a creature affected by your Emboldening Bond feature is about to take damage, a second bonded creature within 30 feet of the first can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to teleport to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the first creature. The second creature then takes all the damage instead.

features.name: Expansive Bond
features.description: The benefits of your Emboldening Bond and Protective Bond features now work when the creatures are within 60 feet of each other. Moreover, when a creature uses Protective Bond to take someone else's damage, the creature has resistance to that damage.
